value.execution:
<!-- Append-only run record (ADR-0103). -->

- **2026-06-26 â€” v0 shipped** (corpus-cli `f53da68`, kit `cc037cc`). `corpus check --staleness` diffs
  each snapshotted spec's Affected areas against its `snapshot:` SHA and flags drift. Covers **AC-001**
  (D1 â†’ frontmatter `snapshot:`, see above), **AC-002** (the `--staleness` advisory), **AC-003** (names
  the drifted areas + the short SHA), **AC-004** (draft specs skipped), **AC-005** (advisory only â€” no
  `checks.yaml`, opt-in flag, never blocking).
  - D2 â†’ **(a) Affected-areas paths** (recommended); D3 â†’ **(a) any change = stale** (recommended).
  - Affected areas: `corpus-cli` â€” `parseSpecRecord` (`snapshot:` field), `Workspace/git`
    (`paths_changed_since`), new `Core/scanSpecStaleness`, `Commands/check` (`--staleness`); `kit` +
    `corpus-works` spec templates (the `snapshot:` convention).
  - Verify results: `pnpm typecheck` âś“ Â· `pnpm vitest run --coverage` 801 tests âś“ (98.52/94.26/99.54/98.51)
    Â· `pnpm lint` âś“ Â· `pnpm deps:validate` âś“ (92 modules). 9 new tests (6 use-case over a real git repo, 3 command).
  - **0-FP by construction:** draft / no-snapshot / unresolvable-SHA / no-git / cross-root-area all skip.
  - **Deferred:** the **cross-root** case (a spec whose areas live in a sibling repo â€” the corpus-works
    dogfood layout) needs per-area repo resolution â†’ tracked under corpus-cli#2. The convention also
    needs the spec authoring guide to instruct stamping the SHA on amendment (follow-up).
  - Spec now `active` (shipped). D1 resolved as the frontmatter `snapshot:` field; the owner may amend
    to the `## Execution`-SHA form (option b) later as a living-spec change.
- **2026-06-26 â€” now LIVE: `corpus stamp` shipped** (corpus-cli `c04d309`). `corpus stamp <spec>` writes
  `snapshot:` = the code repo's HEAD, so staleness detection is no longer dormant â€” stamp a spec, then
  `corpus check --staleness` flags it when its areas drift. (D1 = frontmatter `snapshot:`, owner-confirmed.)
